question: Explain why propan-2-ol is the major product when propene reacts by electrophilic addition.
prompt: The secondary carbocation is more stable than the primary carbocation. This is because two alkyl groups have a greater positive inductive effect than _____ alkyl group. Therefore the pathway through the secondary carbocation is favoured.
full_answer: The secondary carbocation is more stable than the primary carbocation. This is because two alkyl groups have a greater positive inductive effect than one alkyl group. Therefore the pathway through the secondary carbocation is favoured.

question: Explain why propan-2-ol is formed in a greater yield than propan-1-ol when propene is hydrated.
prompt: Propan-2-ol is formed via the more stable _____ carbocation. The secondary carbocation is stabilised by the positive inductive effect of the two alkyl groups, so that pathway is favoured.
full_answer: Propan-2-ol is formed via the more stable secondary carbocation. The secondary carbocation is stabilised by the positive inductive effect of the two alkyl groups, so that pathway is favoured.
